summary: Middle East conflict risk (Strait of Hormuz closure threat), U.S.-Venezuela tensions, NATO-Greenland posture, ongoing tariff uncertainty under Trump, and mid-term elections. IMF downgraded global growth to 3.1% with headline inflation at 4.4% under reference scenario (19% energy price spike). Despite risks, S&P 500 +4.5% and Nasdaq 100 +6.2% hit fresh all-time highs as AI-driven investment offsets geopolitical drag.
